heading: Essential health benefits requirements
label: 18022 Essential health benefits requirements

children.text: provides for the essential health benefits defined by the Secretary under subsection (b);
limits cost-sharing for such coverage in accordance with subsection (c); and
subject to subsection (e), provides either the bronze, silver, gold, or platinum level of coverage described in subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: Ambulatory patient services.

children.label: B
children.text: Emergency services.

children.label: C
children.text: Hospitalization.

children.label: D
children.text: Maternity and newborn care.

children.label: E
children.text: Mental health and substance use disorder services, including behavioral health treatment.

children.label: F
children.text: Prescription drugs.

children.label: G
children.text: Rehabilitative and habilitative services and devices.

children.label: H
children.text: Laboratory services.

children.label: I
children.text: Preventive and wellness services and chronic disease management.

children.label: J
children.text: Pediatric services, including oral and vision care.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the scope of the essential health benefits under paragraph (1) is equal to the scope of benefits provided under a typical employer plan, as determined by the Secretary. To inform this determination, the Secretary of Labor shall conduct a survey of employer-sponsored coverage to determine the benefits typically covered by employers, including multiemployer plans, and provide a report on such survey to the Secretary.

children.heading: Certification
children.label: B Certification
children.text: In defining the essential health benefits described in paragraph (1), and in revising the benefits under paragraph (4)(H), the Secretary shall submit a report to the appropriate committees of Congress containing a certification from the Chief Actuary of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services that such essential health benefits meet the limitation described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Notice and hearing
children.label: 3 Notice and hearing
children.text: In defining the essential health benefits described in paragraph (1), and in revising the benefits under paragraph (4)(H), the Secretary shall provide notice and an opportunity for public comment.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure that such essential health benefits reflect an appropriate balance among the categories described in such subsection, so that benefits are not unduly weighted toward any category;2So in original. Probably should be “paragraph,”.2

children.label: B
children.text: not make coverage decisions, determine reimbursement rates, establish incentive programs, or design benefits in ways that discriminate against individuals because of their age, disability, or expected length of life;

children.label: C
children.text: take into account the health care needs of diverse segments of the population, including women, children, persons with disabilities, and other groups;

children.label: D
children.text: ensure that health benefits established as essential not be subject to denial to individuals against their wishes on the basis of the individuals’ age or expected length of life or of the individuals’ present or predicted disability, degree of medical dependency, or quality of life;

children.label: i
children.text: coverage for emergency department services will be provided without imposing any requirement under the plan for prior authorization of services or any limitation on coverage where the provider of services does not have a contractual relationship with the plan for the providing of services that is more restrictive than the requirements or limitations that apply to emergency department services received from providers who do have such a contractual relationship with the plan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if such services are provided out-of-network, the cost-sharing requirement (expressed as a copayment amount or coinsurance rate) is the same requirement that would apply if such services were provided in-network;

children.label: F
children.text: provide that if a plan described in section 18031(b)(2)(B)(ii) of this title (relating to stand-alone dental benefits plans) is offered through an Exchange, another health plan offered through such Exchange shall not fail to be treated as a qualified health plan solely because the plan does not offer coverage of benefits offered through the stand-alone plan that are otherwise required under paragraph (1)(J); and34So in original. Probably should be “18031(d)(2)(B)(ii)”.3So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.4

children.label: i
children.text: an assessment of whether enrollees are facing any difficulty accessing needed services for reasons of coverage or cost;

children.label: ii
children.text: an assessment of whether the essential health benefits needs to be modified or updated to account for changes in medical evidence or scientific advancement;

children.label: iii
children.text: information on how the essential health benefits will be modified to address any such gaps in access or changes in the evidence base;

children.label: iv
children.text: an assessment of the potential of additional or expanded benefits to increase costs and the interactions between the addition or expansion of benefits and reductions in existing benefits to meet actuarial limitations described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: H
children.text: periodically update the essential health benefits under paragraph (1) to address any gaps in access to coverage or changes in the evidence base the Secretary identifies in the review conducted under subparagraph (G).

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 5 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this title shall be construed to prohibit a health plan from providing benefits in excess of the essential health benefits described in this subsection.1

children.label: A 2014
children.text: The cost-sharing incurred under a health plan with respect to self-only coverage or coverage other than self-only coverage for a plan year beginning in 2014 shall not exceed the dollar amounts in effect under for self-only and family coverage, respectively, for taxable years beginning in 2014.section 223(c)(2)(A)(ii) of title 26

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of self-only coverage, be equal to the dollar amount under subparagraph (A) for self-only coverage for plan years beginning in 2014, increased by an amount equal to the product of that amount and the premium adjustment percentage under paragraph (4) for the calendar year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of other coverage, twice the amount in effect under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: deductibles, coinsurance, copayments, or similar charges; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any other expenditure required of an insured individual which is a qualified medical expense (within the meaning of ) with respect to essential health benefits covered under the plan.section 223(d)(2) of title 26

children.heading: Exceptions
children.label: B Exceptions
children.text: Such term does not include premiums, balance billing amounts for non-network providers, or spending for non-covered services.

children.heading: Premium adjustment percentage
children.label: 4 Premium adjustment percentage
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(B)(i), the premium adjustment percentage for any calendar year is the percentage (if any) by which the average per capita premium for health insurance coverage in the United States for the preceding calendar year (as estimated by the Secretary no later than October 1 of such preceding calendar year) exceeds such average per capita premium for 2013 (as determined by the Secretary).

children.heading: Bronze level
children.label: A Bronze level
children.text: A plan in the bronze level shall provide a level of coverage that is designed to provide benefits that are actuarially equivalent to 60 percent of the full actuarial value of the benefits provided under the plan.

children.heading: Silver level
children.label: B Silver level
children.text: A plan in the silver level shall provide a level of coverage that is designed to provide benefits that are actuarially equivalent to 70 percent of the full actuarial value of the benefits provided under the plan.

children.heading: Gold level
children.label: C Gold level
children.text: A plan in the gold level shall provide a level of coverage that is designed to provide benefits that are actuarially equivalent to 80 percent of the full actuarial value of the benefits provided under the plan.

children.heading: Platinum level
children.label: D Platinum level
children.text: A plan in the platinum level shall provide a level of coverage that is designed to provide benefits that are actuarially equivalent to 90 percent of the full actuarial value of the benefits provided under the plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Under regulations issued by the Secretary, the level of coverage of a plan shall be determined on the basis that the essential health benefits described in subsection (b) shall be provided to a standard population (and without regard to the population the plan may actually provide benefits to).

children.heading: Employer contributions
children.label: B Employer contributions
children.text: The Secretary shall issue regulations under which employer contributions to a health savings account (within the meaning of ) may be taken into account in determining the level of coverage for a plan of the employer.section 223 of title 26

children.heading: Application
children.label: C Application
children.text: In determining under this title, the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.], or title 26 the percentage of the total allowed costs of benefits provided under a group health plan or health insurance coverage that are provided by such plan or coverage, the rules contained in the regulations under this paragraph shall apply.142 U.S.C. 201

children.heading: Allowable variance
children.label: 3 Allowable variance
children.text: The Secretary shall develop guidelines to provide for a de minimis variation in the actuarial valuations used in determining the level of coverage of a plan to account for differences in actuarial estimates.

children.heading: Plan reference
children.label: 4 Plan reference
children.text: In this title, any reference to a bronze, silver, gold, or platinum plan shall be treated as a reference to a qualified health plan providing a bronze, silver, gold, or platinum level of coverage, as the case may be.1

children.label: A
children.text: the only individuals who are eligible to enroll in the plan are individuals described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: i
children.text: except as provided in clause (ii), the essential health benefits determined under subsection (b), except that the plan provides no benefits for any plan year until the individual has incurred cost-sharing expenses in an amount equal to the annual limitation in effect under subsection (c)(1) for the plan year (except as provided for in section 2713); and1

children.label: ii
children.text: coverage for at least three primary care visits.

children.label: A
children.text: has not attained the age of 30 before the beginning of the plan year; or

children.label: i
children.text: section 5000A(e)(1) of such title (relating to individuals without affordable coverage); or

children.label: ii
children.text: section 5000A(e)(5) of such title (relating to individuals with hardships).

children.heading: Restriction to individual market
children.label: 3 Restriction to individual market
children.text: If a health insurance issuer offers a health plan described in this subsection, the issuer may only offer the plan in the individual market.

children.heading: Child-only plans
children.label: f Child-only plans
children.text: If a qualified health plan is offered through the Exchange in any level of coverage specified under subsection (d), the issuer shall also offer that plan through the Exchange in that level as a plan in which the only enrollees are individuals who, as of the beginning of a plan year, have not attained the age of 21, and such plan shall be treated as a qualified health plan.

children.heading: Payments to Federally-qualified health centers
children.label: g Payments to Federally-qualified health centers
children.text: If any item or service covered by a qualified health plan is provided by a Federally-qualified health center (as defined in section 1396d()(2)(B) of this title) to an enrollee of the plan, the offeror of the plan shall pay to the center for the item or service an amount that is not less than the amount of payment that would have been paid to the center under ) for such item or service.lsection 1396a(bb) of this title
